The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 +

The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 maintenance","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 :"open","locked":false,"title":"Cron maintenance","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 {"action":"opened","number":448,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pulls/448","id":1720012570,"node_id":"PR_kwDOBCYhWs5mhU8a","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pull/448","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pull/448.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pull/448.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/448","number":448,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Cron job maintenance","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

This is untested as of yet.

cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 :"open","locked":false,"title":"Cron maintenance","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/448/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/448/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/comments/1941665110","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/pull/448#issuecomment-1941665110","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/448","id":1941665110,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBCYhWs5zu3VW","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-13T14:42:06Z","updated_at":"2024-02-13T14:42:06Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> what if we just have a single bash script that is invoked by a single cronjob, which will in turn invoke the 3 cleanup commands sequentially? maintenance","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 :"open","locked":false,"title":"Cron maintenance","user":{"login":"ksuderman","id":4098072,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwOTgwNzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4098072?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman","html_url":"https://github.com/ksuderman","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ksuderman/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Adds cron jobs that clean up the `jobs_directory`, datasets, and histories. The jobs are defined in the `values.yaml` file and templates generated from the `cronjob-maintenance.yaml` template.  The cron jobs are based on the [Ansible Playbook used on main](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/main/env/main/host_vars/galaxy-web-05.tacc.utexas.edu.yml)

Since the `/galaxy/server/database/tmp` directory is cleaned up I am not sure the job that cleans the `tus_upload_store` is needed since it also lives in `/galaxy/server/database/tmp`, but I've included it for completeness.

The `pgcleanup.py` script writes logs to the `tmp` directory, but we should likely create a separate logs directory for these and then also prune the logs as needed.

Should we just call the `maintenance.sh` script, or a modified version of the script?

**NOTE** I have set the cron jobs to `runAsUser: 0` but we should probably run these as the Galaxy user once we have determined why `mutliqc` is creating files and directories as `nobody`.

This is untested as of yet.

Closes #408 job priorityClass value is set here to -1000: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/blob/4e12bbb7caee93cd05d997ba93ff33e7a596c74e/galaxy/templates/priorityclass-job.yaml#L8

As the value is very low, k8s does not start new nodes to work these jobs. 

*Use Case*: k8s uses two node groups: one "exclusively" for jobs and one for the web server and other non-performance intensive pods. The job node group consists of powerful machines, which should scale to 0 when no job is running.  Currently, k8s won't spin those machines up, as the priority of the jobs is too low.

*Suggestion* Expose this as variable to allow customizations to allow the increase of the priority for such a use case.
